title: Freudian Theories in Eva
wikitext:
[]: Freudian Theories in Eva...             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sat Mar 03, 2007 1:53 am Just wondering if this topic has been discussed before so i don't waste my time. Its some pretty interesting stuff and has a lot of similar theories that tie in with Eva

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 03, 2007 11:08 am Though there seems to be a lot of Freudian and psychoanalysis references in Evangelion, I'm not sure how closely Evangelion can be tied together using them. It's not clear how much in depth the author read into these topics, or the extent to which events in ther series are shaped by them. I think that there's a lot of psychoanalysis there, but how accurate it is, is entirely another matter. And of course, there's always the question of the accuracy and/or validity of many psychoanalytical theories themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Mar 03, 2007 12:24 pm For the most part, it's been beaten to death, and it's pretty much subjective to the point where it's not even worth arguing. Like, it's there, but it's not something that will aid you in either understanding or appreciating Eva. I think that all you really need to know is that Anno's study of Psycho-analysis aided him in crafting the portions of Evangelion which took the form of experimental narrative rather than a conventional linear narrative.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sat Mar 03, 2007 7:28 pm alright just wondering, my friend claims that after watching 5 minutes of an episode he knows what its all about and that its all based on the theories, i will now proceed to slap him and make him watch the whole thing.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Mar 03, 2007 10:04 pm Erikson > Freud

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 03, 2007 10:13 pm Fearthebait wrote: alright just wondering, my friend claims that after watching 5 minutes of an episode he knows what its all about and that its all based on the theories, i will now proceed to slap him and make him watch the whole thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 03, 2007 11:48 pm lol psychoanalysis That's all I have to say.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sat Mar 03, 2007 11:48 pm when i said 5 minutes i mean he knows what the entire series is about and how to explain it all, hence the slappage.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:25 am I SAW FIVE MINUTES OF EOE NOW I CAN EXPLAIN THE ENTIRE SERIES BECAUSE I WENT TO CATHOLIC SCHOOL

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:41 am If anyone has any links I would actually like to read more about this. NGE is obviously a very psychological series, with a lot of its themes dealing with topics that fall under that category. I imagine a good understanding of different psychological fields might help explain some of the workings behind NGE that many miss...

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:54 am Reichu wrote: I SAW FIVE MINUTES OF EOE NOW I CAN EXPLAIN THE ENTIRE SERIES BECAUSE I WENT TO CATHOLIC SCHOOL

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:56 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: If anyone has any links I would actually like to read more about this. NGE is obviously a very psychological series, with a lot of its themes dealing with topics that fall under that category. I imagine a good understanding of different psychological fields might help explain some of the workings behind NGE that many miss...

NGE is obviously a very psychological series, with a lot of its themes dealing with topics that fall under that category. I imagine a good understanding of different psychological fields might help explain some of the workings behind NGE that many miss...

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:21 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Fearthebait wrote: alright just wondering, my friend claims that after watching 5 minutes of an episode he knows what its all about and that its all based on the theories, i will now proceed to slap him and make him watch the whole thing.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:27 pm SEELE 08 wrote: Yeah I'm a psych minor. Freud made some contributions to pyschology, but most of his theories were nothing but projection of his own issues. Point in case, I do not suffer from "penis envy".

[]:             Quote Post by x1berserk » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:21 pm Fearthebait wrote: alright just wondering, my friend claims that after watching 5 minutes of an episode he knows what its all about and that its all based on the theories, i will now proceed to slap him and make him watch the whole thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:37 pm SEELE 08 wrote: Yeah I'm a psych minor. Freud made some contributions to pyschology, but most of his theories were nothing but projection of his own issues. Point in case, I do not suffer from "penis envy".

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 04, 2007 2:34 pm Reichu wrote: I, on the other hand, suffer from castration anxiety.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 04, 2007 8:03 pm are you insinuating reichu is what i think you are thinking?

It's not clear how much in depth the author read into these topics, or the extent to which events in ther series are shaped by them. I think that there's a lot of psychoanalysis there, but how accurate it is, is entirely another matter. And of course, there's always the question of the accuracy and/or validity of many psychoanalytical theories themselves.
